title: Sticky notes in dynamic size mode show shadow artifact and oversized caret at high zoom
body:
## Description

When using dynamic size mode, sticky notes at very high zoom levels (e.g. 800%) exhibit two visual issues:

1. **Shadow artifact above the note** — A shadow appears above the sticky note that shouldn't be there
2. **Oversized caret** — The text editing caret becomes disproportionately large when typing in the note

## Steps to reproduce

1. Enable dynamic size mode (View menu → Dynamic size mode)
2. Create a sticky note
3. Zoom in to ~800%
4. Observe the shadow rendering — there is a shadow artifact visible above the note
5. Double-click the note to edit text — the caret is oversized

## Expected behavior

- Shadows should render cleanly around the note without artifacts at any zoom level
- The text caret should be proportionally sized to the text/note at all zoom levels

## Actual behavior

- A shadow appears above the sticky note at high zoom
- The caret is disproportionately large when editing text in the note

## Likely affected areas

- Shadow calculation in `NoteShapeUtil.tsx` (`getNoteShadow()`) — shadow offsets/blur scale with `scale`, which in dynamic size mode equals `1/zoom`. At 800% zoom, the scale is very small (0.125), which may cause the shadow math to produce negative offsets that render above the note.
- Caret sizing may not be accounting for the note's `scale` prop correctly — the caret inherits from the container's font size or CSS context rather than the scaled font size.
